title: Everyday Life English
description: Practical vocabulary for daily routines, household life, relationships, emotions, food, health, and community living.

cards.meaning: food and other goods bought at a supermarket
cards.example: I need to pick up groceries on my way home.

cards.meaning: clothes that need to be or have been washed
cards.example: I do the laundry every Saturday morning.

cards.meaning: the journey between home and work each day
cards.example: My commute takes about 45 minutes each way.

cards.meaning: an arranged meeting at a specific time
cards.example: I have a dentist appointment at three o'clock.

cards.meaning: a written instruction from a doctor for medicine
cards.example: I need to collect my prescription from the pharmacy.

cards.meaning: an arrangement to receive money if something bad happens
cards.example: Make sure you have travel insurance before going abroad.

cards.meaning: a loan used to buy a property
cards.example: They took out a mortgage to buy their house.

cards.meaning: regular payment for using someone else's property
cards.example: The rent for the flat is very expensive.

cards.meaning: services such as electricity, water, and gas
cards.example: Utilities are included in the monthly rent.

cards.meaning: a plan for how to spend your money
cards.example: I always stick to a monthly budget.

cards.meaning: money that you keep rather than spend
cards.example: She used her savings to travel around Asia.

cards.meaning: to put money into something to gain profit later
cards.example: He decided to invest in stocks and property.

cards.meaning: to stop working permanently, usually due to age
cards.example: She plans to retire at the age of sixty.

cards.meaning: an activity you do for pleasure in your free time
cards.example: His hobby is photography and hiking.

cards.meaning: free time when you can relax and enjoy yourself
cards.example: Reading is her favourite leisure activity.

cards.meaning: to offer to do something without being paid
cards.example: She volunteers at the local food bank every weekend.

cards.meaning: an organization that helps those in need
cards.example: He donates to a children's charity every month.

cards.meaning: the area around where you live
cards.example: We live in a quiet neighborhood.

cards.meaning: a group of people living in the same area
cards.example: The whole community came together to clean the park.

cards.meaning: informal talk about other people's private lives
cards.example: She doesn't like gossip and prefers to mind her own business.

cards.meaning: a piece of information spread without proof
cards.example: There is a rumor that the company is closing.

cards.meaning: feeling sorry for someone who is suffering
cards.example: I expressed my sympathy when I heard about her loss.

cards.meaning: the ability to understand how others feel
cards.example: Good counsellors show empathy toward their clients.

cards.meaning: an agreement reached by both sides giving up something
cards.example: We reached a compromise after a long discussion.

cards.meaning: to disagree with someone using spoken words
cards.example: They argued about money for hours.

cards.meaning: to say sorry for something you did wrong
cards.example: He apologized for being late to the meeting.

cards.meaning: to stop feeling angry about a wrong someone did
cards.example: She eventually forgave him for his mistake.

cards.meaning: to do something special for a happy occasion
cards.example: We celebrated our anniversary with a nice dinner.

cards.meaning: the yearly return of a memorable date
cards.example: Tomorrow is our fifth wedding anniversary.

cards.meaning: a ceremony held after someone dies
cards.example: Friends and family gathered for the funeral.

cards.meaning: a marriage ceremony
cards.example: Their wedding was held on the beach at sunset.

cards.meaning: the ceremony of completing an academic course
cards.example: My graduation ceremony is next month.

cards.meaning: the anniversary of the day you were born
cards.example: Happy birthday! How old are you today?

cards.meaning: something given to someone as a present
cards.example: She gave me a lovely gift for my birthday.

cards.meaning: something unexpected that causes astonishment
cards.example: We threw a surprise party for her.

cards.meaning: a custom passed down through generations
cards.example: It is a family tradition to have a big dinner on Sundays.

cards.meaning: the shared customs and beliefs of a group of people
cards.example: Understanding local culture helps when travelling.

cards.meaning: feeling sad because you are away from home
cards.example: She felt homesick during her first month abroad.

cards.meaning: a sentimental longing for the past
cards.example: Old songs often trigger feelings of nostalgia.

cards.meaning: a strong desire to achieve something
cards.example: Her ambition is to become a successful writer.

cards.meaning: an exciting or unusual experience
cards.example: Living abroad was a great adventure for him.

cards.meaning: to travel to and examine an unfamiliar area
cards.example: We explored the old part of the city on foot.

cards.meaning: to find or learn something for the first time
cards.example: She discovered a great little cafe near the office.

cards.meaning: travel from one place to another
cards.example: The journey from London to Paris took two hours.

cards.meaning: the place you are travelling to
cards.example: Paris is a popular tourist destination.

cards.meaning: the natural features of a landscape
cards.example: The scenery in the mountains was breathtaking.

cards.meaning: the visible features of an area of land
cards.example: The landscape changed dramatically after the rain.

cards.meaning: the typical weather conditions in an area
cards.example: The Mediterranean climate is warm and sunny.

cards.meaning: a prediction of future weather or events
cards.example: The forecast says it will rain this weekend.

cards.meaning: the amount of moisture in the air
cards.example: The humidity in summer here is very uncomfortable.

cards.meaning: a long period of abnormally low rainfall
cards.example: The drought destroyed many crops this year.

cards.meaning: an overflow of water over normally dry land
cards.example: Heavy rain caused floods in the valley.

cards.meaning: a sudden violent shaking of the ground
cards.example: A strong earthquake hit the region last night.

cards.meaning: the presence of harmful substances in the environment
cards.example: Air pollution is a serious problem in big cities.

cards.meaning: to convert waste into reusable material
cards.example: We recycle glass, paper, and plastic at home.

cards.meaning: able to be maintained without harming the environment
cards.example: We need sustainable energy solutions for the future.

cards.meaning: produced without artificial chemicals or fertilizers
cards.example: She prefers to buy organic vegetables.

cards.meaning: not eating or using any animal products
cards.example: He went vegan for health and ethical reasons.

cards.meaning: a bad reaction of the body to a substance
cards.example: She has a serious allergy to peanuts.

cards.meaning: a sign that shows you may be ill
cards.example: Coughing is a common symptom of a cold.

cards.meaning: the identification of an illness or problem
cards.example: The doctor gave her a diagnosis after running tests.

cards.meaning: treatment for a physical or mental condition
cards.example: He goes to therapy every week to manage anxiety.

cards.meaning: a practice of focusing the mind to achieve calm
cards.example: She practices meditation every morning.

cards.meaning: a physical and mental practice involving stretching and breathing
cards.example: Yoga helps her stay flexible and calm.

cards.meaning: being in good physical health through exercise
cards.example: Regular exercise is important for your fitness.

cards.meaning: the process of eating the right food for good health
cards.example: Good nutrition is essential for a healthy life.

cards.meaning: the kind of food a person regularly eats
cards.example: A balanced diet includes vegetables, fruit, and protein.

cards.meaning: the amount of food given to one person
cards.example: The portions at that restaurant are very generous.

cards.meaning: one of the things used to make a dish
cards.example: Eggs are an important ingredient in this cake.

cards.meaning: instructions for preparing a particular dish
cards.example: She found a delicious recipe online.

cards.meaning: the distinctive taste of a food or drink
cards.example: This soup has a wonderful flavor.

cards.meaning: the feel or consistency of a food
cards.example: The bread has a soft, chewy texture.

cards.meaning: a pleasant and distinctive smell
cards.example: The aroma of fresh coffee filled the kitchen.

cards.meaning: a plant substance used to flavour food
cards.example: She added spice to the curry to make it hotter.

cards.meaning: to cook food over or under direct heat
cards.example: We grilled the fish over an open fire.

cards.meaning: to cook in an oven using dry heat
cards.example: She bakes bread every Sunday.

cards.meaning: to cook food over boiling water
cards.example: Steaming vegetables preserves more nutrients.

cards.meaning: to cook gently just below boiling point
cards.example: Let the soup simmer for twenty minutes.

cards.meaning: to cut into small pieces with a sharp tool
cards.example: Chop the onions before adding them to the pan.

cards.meaning: to cut into thin, flat pieces
cards.example: Slice the bread before serving.

cards.meaning: to mix ingredients together smoothly
cards.example: Blend the fruit until smooth for the smoothie.

cards.meaning: to add salt, spices, or herbs to food
cards.example: Season the chicken with salt and pepper.

cards.meaning: to decorate a dish with a small amount of extra food
cards.example: Garnish the soup with fresh parsley.

cards.meaning: to put food or drink in front of someone
cards.example: Serve the pasta while it is still hot.

cards.meaning: a desire for food
cards.example: Exercise always increases my appetite.

cards.meaning: strong desires for a particular food or thing
cards.example: She had strong cravings for chocolate during her pregnancy.

cards.meaning: a short trip to do a task, such as shopping
cards.example: I have a few errands to run before lunch.

cards.meaning: a routine household task
cards.example: Doing the dishes is my least favourite chore.

cards.meaning: a person who rents a room or property
cards.example: The new tenant moved in last weekend.

cards.meaning: a person who owns property and rents it to others
cards.example: The landlord agreed to fix the broken window.

cards.meaning: a person walking rather than travelling in a vehicle
cards.example: Drivers must give way to pedestrians at crossings.

cards.meaning: practices to maintain health and prevent disease
cards.example: Good hygiene includes washing your hands regularly.

cards.meaning: feeling or showing thanks
cards.example: I am grateful for all the support I received.

cards.meaning: feeling that there is too much to deal with
cards.example: She felt overwhelmed by the amount of work.

cards.meaning: a regular sequence of actions followed daily
cards.example: My morning routine includes exercise and breakfast.

cards.meaning: to repair and improve a building
cards.example: They renovated the kitchen last summer.

cards.meaning: a device or machine used in the home
cards.example: The washing machine is our most-used appliance.

cards.meaning: giving a feeling of comfort and warmth
cards.example: The living room is small but very cozy.

cards.meaning: a person who regularly travels between home and work
cards.example: The train is packed with commuters every morning.

cards.meaning: a collection of things lying around in an untidy way
cards.example: She cleared the clutter from her desk to focus better.
